title: astelia alpina var. novae-hollandiae
intro: Astelia alpina var. novae-hollandiae, also known as the New Zealand Astelia or Wire Rasp, is a variety of the Astelia alpina plant, native to New Zealand and southeastern Australia. It is a type of perennial plant with striking, silver-gray foliage and tall, slender flower stalks. The Astelia alpina var. novae-hollandiae has been used in traditional Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been shown to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and alleviate respiratory issues. The plant can be prepared into various herbal remedies, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to promote overall health and well-being, while also providing relief from various health conditions.
